query: add role-based access control to the app — I need admin, staff, and member roles with different permission sets
create a requirePermission('booking:create') tRPC middleware that throws FORBIDDEN if the user lacks the permission
build a <RequirePermission permission='admin:access'> wrapper component that hides children from unauthorized users
design the database schema for users, roles, and permissions tables with Drizzle — I need a many-to-many relationship between roles and permissions
my admin user is getting a 403 on the /api/users endpoint even though they should have user:read — how do I debug the permission check?
implement a useRequirePermission hook that redirects to /unauthorized if the current user lacks the specified permission
add a centralized PERMISSIONS constant with resource:action format — booking:read, booking:create, booking:update, booking:delete, admin:access
the UI still shows the 'Delete User' button to staff members even though they don't have user:delete — fix the frontend guard
after changing a user's role from member to admin, the permission change doesn't take effect until they log out and back in
set up authorization middleware that distinguishes between 401 (not logged in) and 403 (logged in but no permission) for the booking API
add a login form with email and password fields using TanStack Form and shadcn Input components
implement JWT token generation and validation in the ElysiaJS authentication endpoint
add a beforeLoad guard on the /_auth layout route that redirects to /login if there's no session
set up OAuth2 with Google sign-in using the googleapis package in the backend
create a pgTable for user_sessions with columns id, user_id, token, and expires_at
hash the user password with bcrypt before storing it in the database
add rate limiting to the /api/login endpoint to prevent brute force attacks
set up CORS headers on the ElysiaJS server to allow requests from localhost:3000
implement row-level security in PostgreSQL so users can only see their own records
add Content-Security-Policy headers to the Cloudflare Workers response
